hub: kosha (Gasuns Sanskrit Dictionary) — the Sanskrit data-hub
note_for_agents: This is the ONE machine-readable list of canonical derived Sanskrit datasets across the ~85 repos. Before deriving an alignment/frequency/headword/crosswalk asset, check here. tier=public assets are downloadable from the interim_release URL; tier=restricted assets exist but are not published (rights-encumbered or unbackuped local-only) — consult their source repo; tier=intermediate is raw/working data that feeds another registered dataset — not standalone-citable, not rights-gated, just not a finished asset in its own right (excluded from the public directory page). A session that creates or changes a derived dataset MUST add/update its row here in the same pass.

datasets.title: MW uttarapada index × DCS Kompozity — dictionary productivity vs corpus attestation
datasets.keying: final_member (MW uttarapada, orthography-folded: anusvāra ṃ/ṁ, avagraha, @/- markup) -> dictionary side (mw_class ∈ UTTARAPADA/KRT_STEM_MEMBER, mw_first_members = distinct-first-member TYPE count) vs corpus side (corpus_compounds = distinct attested word-forms, corpus_tokens = summed DCS frequency, corpus_first_members) + divergence cols (overlap_first, mw_only_first, corpus_only_first) + corpus_status ∈ {final 6249, form_variant 1289, nonfinal_only 1252, absent 10387}
consumers: kosha samāsa trainer member_side/member_recall drill ranking (scripts/build_samasa_trainer.py, --mw-rank corpus, default since H1398 20-07-2026)
datasets.notes: H1328 (20-07-2026, Opus 4.8 claude-opus-4-8). Corpus side is the sibling of dcs-compound-dictionary (same Kompozity cmps.csv/names.csv), joined against the MW dictionary side (MWderivations issue15 reverse-classified index, master branch). corpus_tokens (summed DCS frequency) and corpus_compounds (distinct attested forms) are the corpus-attestation ranking signal for compound final members; corpus_status=final (6,249) is the drillable stratum, absent (10,387) is a lower bound on real absence, not a ghost-word verdict. Corpus head carries its own junk (ca/eva/pronoun-stems/bare-roots/-tva/-tā) that ranking by tokens alone does not drop — an explicit stoplist is required (report § "corpus's own junk head"). DCS is CC BY 4.0; MW public domain; ships BY-SA with the public tier. H1398 (20-07-2026, Sonnet 5 claude-sonnet-5): kosha's samāsa trainer now consumes this dataset by default (member_stoplist applied per the report's finding above).

datasets.title: Bloomfield 1906 Vedic Concordance — direct Ṛgveda citations (pratīka index)
datasets.keying: maṇḍala + sūkta + verse + pada_letter (a/b/c/d, blank if unlettered) → pratīka text + full raw citation string
datasets.notes: Every direct RV.<mandala>.<sukta>.<verse><pada-letter> citation extracted from Marco Franceschini's digital edition of Maurice Bloomfield's A Vedic Concordance (Harvard Oriental Series 9, 1906) — 88,835 total concordance entries scanned (the concordance indexes the WHOLE Vedic corpus, not just RV), 10,374 distinct RV (mandala,sukta,verse) keys cited. Used to populate parallel_passage_verses.tsv's bloomfield_pratika column for the RV subset (13,581 rows): 11,522 (85%) validated by substring-matching the Bloomfield pratika (parenthetical variant-reading asides stripped, form_key()-normalized, final character dropped to tolerate the pausa-vs-sandhi boundary mismatch) against the row's own recorded source_text — NOT positionally assumed (the row-pair layout is a printed first-half/second-half split, which does not line up 1:1 with Bloomfield's grammatical a/b/c/d pada lettering for 4-pada meters; naive positional mapping would have silently mismatched). Remaining 15% residue is genuine orthographic variance between the two independently-produced digitizations (anusvara-vs-homorganic-nasal spelling, consonant-doubling variants), documented not chased further — full method + counts in data/concordance/BLOOMFIELD_RV_CROSSREF_REPORT.md. RIGHTS: public tier by direct written permission from Marco Franceschini (University of Bologna), non-exclusive/worldwide/perpetual, covering all current and future kosha versions — see data/manifest/rights/franceschini_hos9_permission_2026-07-13.md and LICENSE-DATA.md. Required citation: "Digitization by Marco Franceschini (University of Bologna), of Maurice Bloomfield's A Vedic Concordance (Harvard Oriental Series 9, 1906). Used with permission."

datasets.notes: H949 kosha pedagogy Wave 2 (W2a), the difficulty spine. ONE estimator, not 'the' difficulty (R5): four corpus-grounded axes -- vocab-frequency x sandhi-density x morphology-rarity x compound-load -- documented in METHODS.md and honestly limited (frequency != learnability, R6; sandhi is a boundary-fusion proxy). Scores only packs carrying UD morphology; the 18 Gita packs (built by build_reading_pack_gita.py from a non-UD source) get a SEPARATE reduced 3-axis ordering instead -- see gita-reading-pack-difficulty (H977) -- never fabricated into this 4-axis score. datasets.title: Gita reading-pack difficulty -- reduced 3-axis ordering (W2a follow-up)
datasets.keying: one row per Gita chapter pack, ascending difficulty: order, slug, difficulty (reduced composite in [0,1]), vocab, sandhi, compound (three per-axis loads), content_tokens, tokens, sentences, title. The Gita packs carry no UD morphology, so they are scored on THREE axes -- vocab (slp1 -> lemma_frequency, non-compound content lemmas), sandhi (fraction of tokens carrying the pack's own per-token induced junction rule -- a real sandhi signal, NOT the 4-axis boundary proxy), compound (hyphen-lemma share) -- with the morphology weight dropped and the rest renormalised. Companion gita_reading_pack_difficulty.json + a labelled section on reading/difficulty/index.html.
consumers: graded-reader auto-levelling within the Gita (once UD morphology is added, these fold into the 4-axis score)

datasets.title: Reading-pack per-verse metre annotation (W3a)
datasets.keying: one row per reading-pack sentence: pack, locus, dcs, syllables, metre, metre_type (vrtta/jati), method, confidence, text-preview. Two-tier + null method: strict vrtta via vidyut.chandas (method=vidyut-chandas, confidence=high, requires >=8 syllables); anustubh via a syllable heuristic (method=syllable-heuristic, confidence=medium -- every such tag is a whole-pada multiple of 8 in [8,32], and 840/840 land at exactly 16 = a half-sloka); everything else left unresolved with an empty metre (prose, headings, fragments -- never guessed). Companion metre_coverage.tsv (per-pack distribution + identified %).
consumers: SanskritKaraoke (metre trainer UI -- the consuming surface; kosha emits the data layer only)
reading packs (per-verse metre for graded reading, field 3.9)

datasets.title: Handoff lifecycle gold standard (170 adversarially-verified verdicts)
datasets.keying: H### id → verdict (ARCHIVE_AS_DONE/RERUN_NEEDED/RETIRE_AS_DEAD/RUNNABLE_NOW/GENUINELY_BLOCKED/NEEDS_STUB_FILL/UNCLEAR) + classifier verdict + adversarial-verifier verdict (upheld/refuted/uncontested)
datasets.builder: tools/build_handoff_gold_set.py (35 batch classifiers on Opus 4.8 + per-verdict adversarial verifier, H1251)
datasets.notes: H1251 (18-07-2026, Haiku 4.5 claude-haiku-4-5-20251001): 170 active handoffs (all in Uprava/handoffs/ as of 18-07-2026) classified + adversarially verified. Audit method: 35 batch classifiers (Opus 4.8) run independently on each handoff's body, each verdict re-checked by an independent adversarial verifier; verifier wins on refutation. Verdict breakdown: ARCHIVE_AS_DONE 57 · GENUINELY_BLOCKED 53 · NEEDS_STUB_FILL 4 · RERUN_NEEDED 22 · RETIRE_AS_DEAD 12 · RUNNABLE_NOW 22. Verification stats: 91 upheld · 15 refuted · 64 uncontested. 64 unverified verdicts reflect the audit's sampling strategy (only verdicts of consequence were re-checked); gold set is a baseline, not scripture — a later handoff correcting a gold row with evidence is legitimate and must be logged.

datasets.title: Generated↔attested morphology audit (A3, W1b)
datasets.keying: kosha.db generated inflected forms (forms, include_heritage=False; 426,410 non-heritage rows) joined to DCS attested surface forms (dcs_full.sqlite token.form; 381,413 distinct) on form_key() equality — the length-preserving floor tier, promoted to exact where SLP1 keys are byte-identical (no NFD+strip path, D6/1b-2). Three buckets: AG 401,368 (of 426,410 generated) · G¬A 25,042 · A¬G 2 (of 381,413 attested). Canonical concordance-core schema (anchor_type/anchor_id/anchor_key_slp1/target_locus/match_method/confidence/evidence_count) + gen_source, tense_caveat, attested_form. Confidence from TIER_CONFIDENCE (exact 0.95 · floor 0.85). Loci are host-independent dcs:<sent_id>.
datasets.notes: A3 = the artefact A4 cannot start without (D12). Sidecars in data/concordance/: morph_attest_GnA.tsv (over-generation), morph_attest_AnG.tsv (engine gap), MORPHOLOGY_ATTESTATION_BUILD_REPORT.md. CIRCULARITY: 93.30% of the generated side is itself source='dcs' (ingested DCS surface tokens), so its AG is a 99.99% round-trip; the research-meaningful figure is the vidyut-engine subtotal — AG 3,550 / 28,567 = 12.43% attested, 25,017 over-generated. Consequently A¬G is degenerate (=2: oṃ + one OCR token) and CANNOT measure engine gaps here — reported as the finding, not hidden (genuine_engine_gap=0, nothing routed to the csl-inflect give-back). CONTRADICTION surfaced (STOP-AND-SURFACE, not resolved): the plan set's 6.9M generated figure is `inflections` (6,917,018 rows), not `forms` (1,378,401) — built on forms per the deliverable text; A4's generated-side table is a human call. Read-only build. PUBLICATION GATED on the W1a rights record (H1263 / D2): unreleased until the DCS BY-SA/BY-4.0 licence contradiction is settled.

datasets.title: vidyut-prakriya derivation harness over the AG bucket (A4, W2a)
datasets.keying: For every W1b AG-bucket row (lemma_slp1 + attested_form), resolves candidate grammatical cells from kosha.db inflections (nominal: gender/gcase/number where person IS NULL; verbal: model/tense/voice/person/number where person IS NOT NULL, v_p passive borrowing gaṇa from the same root's v_<gana> model per H855), derives every candidate cell with vidyut.prakriya (local library, no network — R12), and classifies the form: ok (exactly one distinct ordered-sūtra chain form_key-matches, exact 0.95 outranks floor 0.85 — TIER_CONFIDENCE, never a literal/1.0) · ambiguous (>1 distinct chain matches) · engine-error (candidate cells existed, none derivable) · no-derivation (no cell exists, or cells ran but none matched). derivation_chains.tsv is the (chain_id, step_index)->(source, sutra_code, step_result) sidecar the chain_id in derivation_status.tsv points into.
consumers: W3a sūtra-coverage map (dark-sūtra classification, not yet built)
datasets.notes: Full run over all 401,368 AG rows (883.8s, 454.1 forms/s, cached per-lemma vidyut derivation — 90,690 distinct lemmas). Status distribution: ok 72,764 (18.13%) · no-derivation 237,447 (59.16%) · ambiguous 86,857 (21.64%) · engine-error 4,300 (1.07%) — all four buckets always reported (2a-4), never only successes. 2,815 distinct sūtra chains recorded; chain length min 6 / median 12 / max 37 among ok rows. R-C3: engine-error is dominated by the verbal dhātu+gaṇa+lakāra mapping (new work here; the nominal mapping is E1-proven). R-C4: 256 ok rows are verb-derived AND carry tense_caveat=1 (DCS Tense=Past conflates aorist/perfect) — sūtra attribution for tense-specific rules on those rows may be off, flagged not silently counted. Full detail + 30-example sampled human-verification section: data/concordance/DERIVATION_HARNESS_BUILD_REPORT.md. Deliberately excludes W2b's sūtra->form inversion and the /concordance/panini/ web page — out of scope for this handoff.

datasets.title: Pāṇinian sūtra ↔ DCS corpus concordance (A4, W2b)
datasets.keying: Inverts W2a's derivation_status.tsv (ok-status forms only — see notes) into one row per (sūtra, form, locus) triple: for every ok-status AG-bucket form, walks its ordered Ashtadhyayi-only sūtra chain (chain_id -> derivation_chains.tsv) and emits a concordance row per chain step. Canonical concordance-core schema (anchor_type=panini-sutra, anchor_id=sutra:<a.p.n>, anchor_key_slp1 empty, target_locus=dcs:<sent_id>[_<sub>], source_dataset=dcs, match_method in {exact,floor}, confidence from TIER_CONFIDENCE, evidence_count=1) + form_key_slp1, dcs_text, chain_position, chain_length, chain_id, derivation_status, tense_caveat.
consumers: W3a sūtra-coverage map (dark-sūtra classification, not yet built)
datasets.notes: 893,482 rows from 72,764 ok-status forms across 221 distinct sūtras (of ~4,000 in the Aṣṭādhyāyī) spanning 7/8 adhyāyas (adhyāya 5: no exemplar in this build). Chain length (Ashtadhyayi-only steps) min 6 / median 12 / max 36. Per-sūtra ambiguity rate (2b-6, lemma-attributed — data/concordance/panini_ambiguity_by_sutra.tsv, 221 rows): median 27.1%, range 0.0-69.2%. tense_caveat=1 on 466 of the inverted ok forms, propagated verbatim (2b-5). PARKED GAP (documented, not hidden): exit-check 2b-1 asks for `ok`/`ambiguous` forms both inverted; W2a's shipped derivation_status.tsv carries an EMPTY chain_id for all 86,857 ambiguous rows (verified exhaustively — 0 exceptions), diverging from ARCHITECTURE_KOSHA_CONCORDANCE_Q3.md §4's stated 'records all of them' design. Re-deriving with vidyut to recover ambiguous-form chains was out of scope (W2a's output is this build's entire input per H1390). Follow-up: extend build_panini_derivations.py to record the tied candidate chain_id set for ambiguous rows so a future W2b re-run can invert them too. 165 of 52,025 derivation_chains.tsv steps (Dhatupatha/Varttika/Kaumudi, 0.32%) are excluded from the inversion — not Pāṇini's own Ashtadhyayi sūtras, don't fit anchor_id's regex. Full detail: data/concordance/PANINI_BUILD_REPORT.md.

datasets.title: Inline Sanskrit→Russian gloss layer (reading packs, W-RU-a)
datasets.keying: one row per (reading pack, sentence, token index); joins each pack token to the three SanskritRussian PUBLIC site-tier layers — surface (token slp1 → surface_glossary), lemma (lemma slp1 → lemma_glossary), root (lemma → dcs_lemma2root → root_glossary) — emitting surface_ru/lemma_ru/root_ru + a layer_hit provenance column. 95.6% of tokens carry a lemma-layer RU gloss.
datasets.notes: RIGHTS GATE (PLAN decision 14): RU glosses come ONLY from SanskritRussian's public, GitHub-Pages-served surface/lemma/root glossaries; the restricted upstream RussianTranslation/src/corpus_lexicon.jsonl is never read. Additive: the English token `gloss` is untouched; RU lives under token.gloss_ru {surface,lemma,root}. Coverage report: reading/RU_GLOSS_COVERAGE.md — the measured 95.6% shows the public subset suffices (no rights unlock needed). Gītā packs are PARKED (the Bhagavadgītā is absent from the DCS full corpus), so the layer applies to the 5 built packs (nala 1-3, hitopadeśa, kirātārjunīya).

Splits shown only where they rebuild the surface via corpus-attested rules (no fabricated signal; 56/1,263 chunks honestly unsplit) — the split/grading TSVs are byte-unchanged from H1279 (test_subhashita_difficulty.py green). gloss.ru now PRESENT: 85.3% of the pack's 1,510 tokens carry a lemma-layer RU gloss (89.1% carry a resolved SLP1 lemma at all) — the residual gap is mostly cheda lemmatization misses on rare/compound-internal forms, not a rights gap (still the SanskritRussian **public site-tier** subset only; corpus_lexicon never read).
